expectations.evidence: Nextcloud at localhost:8080 not running. No browser MCP tools available in eval sandbox. Agent reached Step 5 (launch agent) but could not execute browser testing. The skill correctly set up the attempt but environment was missing.
Same blocker — no browser connection possible. Agent documented the failure clearly but could not perform CRUD testing.

Agent produced structured text analysis including a step-by-step table of what succeeded/failed, but Write tools were denied in the eval sandbox. No report.md was written to disk by the agent itself. Report was reconstructed from agent output.

expectations.text: Does NOT spawn parallel agents in single mode
expectations.evidence: Agent correctly identified mode=single from the prompt and followed the SKILL.md single-mode path ('launch 1 Task agent'). Never attempted to spawn 6 parallel agents. Only 1 agent execution was planned before environment blockers halted progress.

suggestions.reason: This assertion only makes sense when the environment is available. In a CANNOT_TEST scenario, the skill correctly produces a report — just not about test results. Consider splitting into 'Report format is structured markdown' (verifiable always) and 'Report includes feature PASS/FAIL entries' (verifiable only with live env).

eval_feedback.overall: All failures are environment-driven, not skill-driven. The skill followed its instructions correctly. Assertions 1-3 require a live Nextcloud environment to pass — they cannot be verified in this eval context.
